summary: Small documentation maintenance PR updating references to the project Discord channel from `#melee` to `#smash-bros-melee` in contributor-facing GitHub docs.
what_changed: In `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md`, changed the PR notification Discord channel reference from `#melee` to `#smash-bros-melee`.
In `.github/README.md`, changed the support/questions Discord channel reference from `#melee` to `#smash-bros-melee`.
No source code, headers, build files, symbols, or matching logic were changed.
smart_moves: Kept the update narrowly scoped to the two user-facing documentation locations that referenced the old Discord channel name.
Preserved existing wording, links, and Discord invite widget while only changing the channel identifier.
decomp_lessons: Contributor onboarding docs should be kept current because they affect how new decomp contributors ask questions and submit PRs.
Small infrastructure/community naming updates are worth applying consistently across README and CONTRIBUTING files to avoid stale guidance.
naming_conventions: Discord channel references are formatted inline in backticks, e.g. `#smash-bros-melee`.
The project Discord channel formerly documented as `#melee` is now documented as `#smash-bros-melee`.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining references to `#melee` in repository documentation.
Search onboarding docs for stale Discord channel names or invite references.
